title: agathophytum rubrum
intro: Agathophytum rubrum, also known as the Tanacetum parthenium or Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include acting as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic agent. The health benefits of Agathophytum rubrum include reducing fever, relieving migraine and tension headaches, and alleviating menstrual cramps. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is also used in herbal capsules and creams.
